_patch.valueProvided: No more than 2 consecutive training days. Minimum 1 rest day between Tue and Thu (already satisfied). Deload if any main lift fails to progress for 3 consecutive sessions. No training if sleep was under 6 hours the prior night.

title: Training Audit
state: User is requesting an audit or correction of a training system.
Program structure may already exist but must be declared explicitly.
4-day strength split detected: Push / Pull / Legs / Full Body.
Training days: Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday.
Goal: +2.5kg to main lifts every 2 weeks using linear progression.
Training days, exercise selection, volume, frequency, and progression logic may be relevant.
Recovery context such as sleep, soreness, HRV, or fatigue may affect admissibility.
constraints: Preserve declared training structure unless explicitly allowed otherwise.
Do not remove training days unless explicitly allowed.
Do not change exercise placement unless explicitly allowed.
Respect declared recovery limits and progression constraints.
Do not infer readiness or overload from missing data.
If correction is requested, prefer the smallest structure-preserving change.
No more than 2 consecutive training days.
Minimum 1 rest day between Tuesday and Thursday (already satisfied by Wednesday rest).
Deload if any main lift fails to progress for 3 consecutive sessions.
No training if sleep was under 6 hours the prior night.
uncertainties: Recovery data may be incomplete or missing.
No fatigue thresholds or HRV data provided.
Progression failure condition now declared — deload trigger is 3 consecutive failed sessions.

candidates.text: Audit only. Determine whether the program is coherent and faithful to the declared constraints.
Audit plus minimal correction. Preserve training structure while correcting overload, recovery mismatch, or progression drift.
Audit plus derive reusable rule. Identify recurring structural weaknesses and express them as a repeatable training rule.

objective: Determine whether the training system is structurally coherent and compatible with the declared constraints.
Identify progression drift, recovery mismatch, or structural contradictions.
If correction is requested, produce the smallest change that restores admissibility.
structurally coherent program with sustainable linear progression, +2.5kg per main lift every 2 weeks
warnings: Consecutive training days (Mon/Tue) are present. Hard constraint allows up to 2 consecutive — this pairing is admissible.
notes: Primary goal: +2.5kg per 2 weeks, linear progression.
Hard constraints patched in — max 2 consecutive days, deload trigger, sleep floor.
